So a lot of people pay attention to things like the click through rate or the earnings per click or what kind of feedback they got from the list. I mean, to me, none of that really matters. The only thing that really matters is how much revenue does the email bring in. When affiliates are mailing for your offer or if you're a copywriter and you wrote copy for them, the number one thing they care about is how much money does that make in comparison to other offers that they promote. So, focusing on things, EPC is like a big thing that a lot of people will focus on. And to me, it's a misguided metric because it really, the EPC could be really high, but you can still be making less than what you make promoting other offers. So what really matters is how much money they make when they send for you. And then a big thing down here that I want to stress is that your email should be written in a way that other affiliates or cold traffic list can use the same one. So that number is different for every business. When I was running high volume of these, it was much more, we like to be in the positive on them, but we would also go down to about like 20% negative. Like I said, it really depends on the business owner, what they're comfortable with. Obviously, the smartest way business wise is to go as negative as you can tolerate and you acquire as many customers as possible because acquiring a customer is the hardest thing in this business. So if you can do it at a negative, if you can do it at 20% negative, even if you're uncomfortable going negative, like it's a better business decision because you're acquiring a bunch of customers. One thing to keep in mind there too is that there's not a direct correlation between, so let's say you have a CPA of 65 bucks to acquire a customer. If you're spending 75 bucks, it's not usually like a 10% jump or whatever that $10 jump. It's usually actually like sometimes you can acquire double the amount of customers with that $10 jump or triple the amount of customers. So by going maybe let's say 30% negative, you might be able to acquire two times as many customers as you could acquire if you're at break even. ## [42:48 - 45:53]

So let's say you're running on Newsmax twice a month. If you have two email creatives to run instead of one, the life of those creatives is going to last a lot longer. That's a big thing a lot of people don't really think about. Like they'll hire, somebody will hire a copywriter and say, I want you to beat my current control.
